domains.title: Geological Atlas
domains.subtitle: Mountains, tectonic plates, volcanoes, oceans, trenches, islands, peninsulas, and more

items.name: Himalayas
items.description: Highest mountain range. Rising at ~5mm/year from Indian-Eurasian plate collision.
items.details: Area: 595,000 km² | Length: 2,400 km | 14 peaks above 8,000m including Everest (8,849m) | Terrain: Alpine glaciers, deep valleys, high plateaus | Attractions: Everest Base Camp, Annapurna Circuit, K2, Lhotse | Source of Ganges, Indus, Brahmaputra rivers

items.name: Andes
items.description: World's longest continental mountain range at 7,000 km.
items.details: Area: 3.37M km² | Length: 7,000 km through 7 countries | Terrain: Volcanic peaks, altiplano plateaus, salt flats, glaciers | Attractions: Machu Picchu, Salar de Uyuni, Patagonia, Torres del Paine | Highest: Aconcagua (6,961m) | Nazca plate subduction

items.name: Alps
items.description: Europe's major mountain system. Formed by African-Eurasian plate collision.
items.details: Area: 200,000 km² | Length: 1,200 km arc across 8 countries | Terrain: Glacial valleys, Alpine meadows, karst landscapes | Attractions: Matterhorn, Mont Blanc (4,809m), Jungfrau, Dolomites | Source of Rhine, Rhône, Po, Danube rivers | World-class skiing

items.name: Rocky Mountains
items.description: Major North American cordillera stretching 4,800 km.
items.details: Area: 991,000 km² | Length: 4,800 km from BC to New Mexico | Terrain: Rugged peaks, alpine tundra, hot springs, canyons | Attractions: Yellowstone, Glacier National Park, Rocky Mountain NP, Banff | Continental Divide | Rich mineral deposits (gold, silver, copper)

items.name: Ural Mountains
items.description: Traditional boundary between Europe and Asia. Among Earth's oldest mountains.
items.details: Area: 400,000 km² | Length: 2,500 km | Age: 250-300 million years | Terrain: Eroded plateaus, taiga forests, mineral-rich ridges | Attractions: Kachkanar, Iremel, Taganay NP | Rich in iron, copper, gold, platinum, emeralds

items.name: Atlas Mountains
items.description: Northwest African range separating Mediterranean from Sahara.
items.details: Area: 775,000 km² | Length: 2,500 km across Morocco, Algeria, Tunisia | Terrain: Cedar forests, Saharan foothills, limestone gorges | Attractions: Toubkal (4,167m), Todra Gorge, Dades Valley, Berber villages | Snow-capped peaks adjacent to desert

items.name: Caucasus Mountains
items.description: Between Black Sea and Caspian Sea. Contains Europe's highest peak.
items.details: Area: 477,000 km² | Length: 1,100 km | Terrain: Glaciated peaks, alpine meadows, volcanic plateaus | Attractions: Mount Elbrus (5,642m), Kazbek, Svaneti, ski resorts | Extreme linguistic diversity (50+ languages) | Tectonic boundary

items.name: Karakoram
items.description: Home to K2, world's 2nd highest peak. Most glaciated area outside poles.
items.details: Area: 77,000 km² | Length: 500 km | Terrain: Extreme glacial landscapes, 8000m peaks, deep gorges | Attractions: K2 (8,611m), Baltoro Glacier, Concordia, Karakoram Highway | Three-way border: Pakistan, China, India | Siachen Glacier

items.name: Hindu Kush
items.description: 800 km range across Afghanistan and Pakistan.
items.details: Area: 155,000 km² | Length: 800 km | Terrain: Arid peaks, narrow valleys, high passes | Attractions: Tirich Mir (7,708m), Wakhan Corridor, Bamyan Valley | Connects Himalayas to Pamir mountains | Historic Silk Road route

items.name: Appalachian Mountains
items.description: Among the oldest mountains on Earth (~480 million years).
items.details: Area: 528,000 km² | Length: 2,400 km | Terrain: Rounded ridges, deciduous forests, rolling valleys | Attractions: Great Smoky Mountains, Blue Ridge Parkway, Appalachian Trail (3,500 km) | Once as tall as Himalayas | Rich coal deposits shaped US industrialization

items.name: Scandinavian Mountains
items.description: Spine of the Scandinavian Peninsula. Glacially carved landscapes.
items.details: Area: 310,000 km² | Length: 1,700 km | Terrain: Fjords, glacial plateaus, tundra, ice fields | Attractions: Galdhøpiggen (2,469m), Norwegian Fjords, Lofoten, Trolltunga | Deep fjords carved by ice ages | Midnight sun zone

items.name: Drakensberg
items.description: Southern Africa's Great Escarpment. Ancient basalt formations.
items.details: Area: 100,000 km² | Length: 1,000 km | Terrain: Basalt cliffs, grasslands, sandstone caves | Attractions: Amphitheatre, Tugela Falls (2nd tallest), Cathedral Peak, San rock art | Thabana Ntlenyana (3,482m) | UNESCO Heritage

items.name: Southern Alps
items.description: New Zealand's spine. Pacific-Australian plate boundary.
items.details: Area: 36,000 km² | Length: 500 km | Terrain: Active glaciers, fiords, alpine lakes, rainforest | Attractions: Aoraki/Mount Cook (3,724m), Milford Sound, Franz Josef Glacier, Fiordland | Rising at 7mm/year | Extreme uplift zone

items.name: Ring of Fire
items.description: 40,000 km horseshoe of oceanic trenches, volcanic arcs, and seismic belts.
items.details: Area: spans 40,000 km around Pacific | 75% of world's volcanoes, 90% of earthquakes | Terrain: Subduction zones, volcanic island arcs, deep trenches | Attractions: Mount Fuji, Krakatoa, Andes volcanoes, Aleutian Islands | Encircles the Pacific Ocean

items.name: Mid-Atlantic Ridge
items.description: Longest mountain range on Earth (~65,000 km), mostly underwater.
items.details: Length: 65,000 km (mostly submarine) | Divergent boundary, plates separate at 2.5 cm/year | Terrain: Underwater volcanic ridges, hydrothermal vents, rift valleys | Attractions: Iceland (surface expression), Azores, Ascension Island | Creates new ocean floor

items.name: East African Rift
items.description: Continent splitting apart. Will form a new ocean in ~10 million years.
items.details: Length: 3,000 km | Width: 30-60 km | Terrain: Rift valleys, alkaline lakes, volcanic highlands | Attractions: Ngorongoro Crater, Ol Doinyo Lengai, Lake Natron, Great Rift Valley viewpoints | Created Great Rift Valley lakes (Tanganyika, Malawi, Turkana)

items.name: San Andreas Fault
items.description: 1,300 km transform fault. Pacific and North American plates slide past each other.
items.details: Length: 1,300 km | Movement: ~46 mm/year | Terrain: Fault scarps, offset streams, pressure ridges | Attractions: Point Reyes, Carrizo Plain, Wallace Creek | 1906 San Francisco earthquake (M7.9) | 'Big One' overdue (M8+)

items.name: Cascadia Subduction Zone
items.description: Juan de Fuca plate subducting under North America. Megathrust threat.
items.details: Length: 1,000 km from BC to N. California | Terrain: Volcanic arc (Cascades), deep trench | Attractions: Mount Rainier, Mount St. Helens, Crater Lake | Last M9 earthquake in 1700, sent tsunami to Japan | Overdue for major event

items.name: Java Trench
items.description: Deepest point in Indian Ocean (7,725m). Indo-Australian plate subduction.
items.details: Length: 3,200 km | Depth: 7,725m | Terrain: Deep oceanic trench, volcanic island arc | Caused 2004 Boxing Day tsunami (M9.1, 230,000+ deaths) | Sunda megathrust. Highly seismically active

items.name: Alpine Fault
items.description: New Zealand's major transform fault. Pacific-Australian plate boundary.
items.details: Length: 600 km | Movement: 27mm/year | Terrain: Uplifted mountain blocks, fault valleys | Major earthquake every ~300 years, last in 1717 — overdue | Creates Southern Alps uplift

items.name: Dead Sea Transform
items.description: Transform fault from Red Sea to Turkey. African-Arabian plate boundary.
items.details: Length: 1,000 km | Terrain: Rift valley, lowest point on land (-430m), salt deposits | Attractions: Dead Sea, Jordan Rift Valley, Sea of Galilee | Active seismicity, historical earthquakes destroyed ancient cities

items.name: Yellowstone Caldera
items.description: Supervolcano with magma chamber holding ~46,000 km³ of molten rock.
items.details: Area: 72 km × 55 km caldera | Terrain: Geothermal basins, hot springs, geysers, fumaroles | Attractions: Old Faithful, Grand Prismatic Spring, Mammoth Hot Springs | Last eruption 640,000 years ago | Eruption cycle ~600-700K years

items.name: Mount Vesuvius
items.description: Destroyed Pompeii in 79 AD. Most dangerous volcano in Europe.
items.details: Height: 1,281m | Area: 482 km² park | Terrain: Stratovolcano cone, caldera, fertile slopes | Attractions: Pompeii & Herculaneum ruins, crater rim hike, Naples views | 3 million people in danger zone | Last eruption 1944

items.name: Mount Fuji
items.description: Japan's highest peak (3,776m). Active stratovolcano. Cultural icon.
items.details: Height: 3,776m | Base diameter: 40-50 km | Terrain: Perfect conical stratovolcano, lava fields, alpine zone | Attractions: Summit climb (July-Sept), Five Lakes region, hot springs | Sits at triple junction of three tectonic plates | UNESCO Heritage

items.name: Krakatoa
items.description: 1883 eruption was one of the deadliest. Anak Krakatau still active.
items.details: Area: Anak Krakatau ~2 km² (growing) | Terrain: Volcanic island, caldera, ash fields | 1883 explosion heard 4,800 km away, killed 36,000+ | Global temperature dropped 1.2°C | Anak Krakatau (Child of Krakatoa) emerged 1927

items.name: Mount Etna
items.description: Europe's most active volcano. Constantly erupting for 500,000 years.
items.details: Height: 3,357m | Area: 1,190 km² | Terrain: Lava flows, craters, pine forests, vineyards | Attractions: Cable car to summit, Valle del Bove, lava caves | UNESCO site | Fertile volcanic soil supports wine & agriculture

items.name: Mauna Loa
items.description: World's largest active volcano by volume. Hawaiian hotspot.
items.details: Height: 4,169m (9,170m from ocean floor base) | Volume: 75,000 km³ | Terrain: Shield volcano, lava tubes, barren lava fields | Attractions: Hawaii Volcanoes NP, Kilauea, lava viewing | Last eruption 2022 | Hawaiian hotspot chain

items.name: Mount Kilimanjaro
items.description: Africa's highest peak (5,895m). Dormant stratovolcano.
items.details: Height: 5,895m | Area: 1,688 km² park | Terrain: 5 climate zones from rainforest to arctic | Attractions: Summit trek (Uhuru Peak), Shira Plateau, glaciers | Glaciers retreating rapidly (80% lost since 1912) | Free-standing volcanic mountain

items.name: Eyjafjallajökull
items.description: 2010 eruption grounded European air traffic for weeks.
items.details: Height: 1,651m | Area: 100 km² ice cap | Terrain: Glacier-capped stratovolcano, ash plains, waterfalls | Attractions: Seljalandsfoss, Skógafoss nearby, glacier hiking | Part of Iceland's volcanic zone on Mid-Atlantic Ridge | $5B economic impact from 2010 eruption

items.name: Taal Volcano
items.description: One of the world's smallest active volcanoes. Lake within a lake.
items.details: Height: 311m | Area: Taal Lake 234 km² | Terrain: Nested caldera — island in lake in island in lake | Attractions: Volcano Island, Taal Heritage Town, Tagaytay viewpoint | 2020 eruption displaced 376,000 | Philippines' most active volcano

items.name: Mount Erebus
items.description: Antarctica's most active volcano. Southernmost active volcano on Earth.
items.details: Height: 3,794m | Terrain: Ice-covered slopes, persistent lava lake, fumaroles, ice towers | One of ~5 long-lived lava lakes on Earth | Discovered by James Clark Ross in 1841 | McMurdo Station nearby | Research site for extremophile organisms

items.name: Pacific Ocean
items.description: Largest and deepest ocean. Covers more area than all land combined.
items.details: Area: 165.25M km² | Average depth: 4,280m | Deepest: Mariana Trench (10,935m) | Terrain: Abyssal plains, volcanic islands, coral atolls, trenches | Attractions: Great Barrier Reef, Hawaiian Islands, Galápagos, Easter Island | Contains ~25,000 islands

items.name: Atlantic Ocean
items.description: 2nd largest ocean. S-shaped. Separates Old and New Worlds.
items.details: Area: 106.46M km² | Average depth: 3,332m | Terrain: Mid-Atlantic Ridge, continental shelves, abyssal plains | Attractions: Bermuda, Azores, Canary Islands, Caribbean reefs | Gulf Stream carries warm water to Europe | Widening 2.5 cm/year

items.name: Indian Ocean
items.description: 3rd largest ocean. Warmest ocean. Monsoon-driven circulation.
items.details: Area: 70.56M km² | Average depth: 3,741m | Terrain: Mid-ocean ridges, tropical reefs, deep basins | Attractions: Maldives, Seychelles, Mauritius, Zanzibar | Major trade routes (Persian Gulf oil) | Monsoon cycle drives regional climate

items.name: Arctic Ocean
items.description: Smallest and shallowest ocean. Sea ice declining rapidly.
items.details: Area: 14.06M km² | Average depth: 1,205m | Terrain: Polar ice cap, continental shelves, submarine ridges | Losing ice at 13%/decade | Opening new shipping routes (Northern Sea Route) | Polar bear and walrus habitat

items.name: Southern Ocean
items.description: Encircles Antarctica. Strongest ocean current: Antarctic Circumpolar Current.
items.details: Area: 20.33M km² | Officially recognized as 5th ocean in 2021 | Terrain: Pack ice, icebergs, deep basins | ACC moves 130M m³/sec (largest current) | Whale migration routes | Krill biomass: ~500M tonnes

items.name: Mediterranean Sea
items.description: Nearly enclosed sea between Europe, Africa, and Asia. Cradle of civilization.
items.details: Area: 2.5M km² | Max depth: 5,267m | Terrain: Volcanic islands, limestone coasts, deep basins | Attractions: Santorini, Amalfi Coast, Dubrovnik, Greek Islands | Connected to Atlantic only by Gibraltar (14 km wide) | Shrinking as Africa moves north

items.name: Caribbean Sea
items.description: Part of the Atlantic. Major hurricane basin. Rich coral reefs.
items.details: Area: 2.75M km² | Max depth: Cayman Trough (7,686m) | Terrain: Coral reefs, volcanic islands, deep trenches | Attractions: Belize Barrier Reef, US/British Virgin Islands, Curaçao | Mesoamerican Barrier Reef (2nd largest) | Hurricane alley

items.name: South China Sea
items.description: Marginal sea of Pacific. Geopolitically contested. Rich marine biodiversity.
items.details: Area: 3.5M km² | Max depth: 5,559m | Terrain: Coral reefs, atolls, shoals, continental shelf | Oil/gas reserves | Coral Triangle (highest marine biodiversity) | $3.4T annual trade passes through

items.name: Red Sea
items.description: Young ocean being born. Spreading center between Africa and Arabia.
items.details: Area: 438,000 km² | Max depth: 3,040m | Terrain: Coral reefs, salt domes, hot brine pools | Attractions: Dahab, Sharm el-Sheikh, Ras Mohammed, wreck diving | World's northernmost tropical sea | Widening 1-1.5 cm/year

items.name: Black Sea
items.description: Nearly landlocked sea. Anoxic below 150m—dead zone for most life.
items.details: Area: 436,000 km² | Max depth: 2,212m | Terrain: Anoxic deep waters, continental shelves, volcanic mud | Attractions: Crimean coast, Bulgarian Riviera, ancient shipwrecks preserved in anoxic layer | Connected to Mediterranean by Turkish Straits

items.name: Caspian Sea
items.description: World's largest enclosed inland body of water. Technically a lake.
items.details: Area: 371,000 km² | Max depth: 1,025m | Terrain: Brackish water, continental shelf, deep southern basin | Bordered by 5 nations | Oil and gas reserves | Sturgeon & caviar (90% of world's supply) | Shrinking due to evaporation

items.name: Sea of Japan
items.description: Marginal sea between Japan and mainland Asia.
items.details: Area: 978,000 km² | Max depth: 3,742m | Terrain: Deep central basin, continental shelves, volcanic seamounts | Warm Tsushima Current | Rich squid and crab fisheries | Name disputed (East Sea by Korea)

items.name: Coral Sea
items.description: Home to the Great Barrier Reef, largest living structure on Earth.
items.details: Area: 4.79M km² | Max depth: 9,140m (New Britain Trench) | Terrain: Coral reefs, atolls, deep basins, sandy cays | Attractions: Great Barrier Reef (2,300 km, visible from space), Osprey Reef, Cod Hole | Coral bleaching threat from warming

items.name: Arabian Sea
items.description: Northwestern Indian Ocean. Major shipping lanes.
items.details: Area: 3.86M km² | Max depth: 5,803m | Terrain: Continental shelf, deep abyssal plain, submarine fans | Ancient trade routes (Silk Road maritime) | Monsoon-driven upwelling creates rich fisheries | Connects to Persian Gulf and Red Sea

items.name: Bay of Bengal
items.description: Largest bay in the world. Cyclone-prone.
items.details: Area: 2.17M km² | Max depth: 4,694m | Terrain: River deltas (Ganges-Brahmaputra, largest on Earth), deep basin | Attractions: Andaman & Nicobar Islands, Sundarbans | Most cyclone-prone body of water | Feeds world's largest mangrove forest

items.name: Baltic Sea
items.description: Brackish inland sea of Northern Europe. Low salinity.
items.details: Area: 377,000 km² | Max depth: 459m | Terrain: Shallow seas, archipelagos, river estuaries, amber coasts | Frozen in winter (north) | Amber deposits | Nord Stream pipelines | 9 bordering countries

items.name: Bering Sea
items.description: Connects Pacific to Arctic. Land bridge during ice ages.
items.details: Area: 2M km² | Max depth: 4,773m | Terrain: Continental shelf, deep basins, volcanic islands (Aleutians) | Richest fishing grounds (pollock, crab) | Bering Strait only 85 km wide | Beringia land bridge during glacial periods

items.name: Lake Baikal
items.description: World's deepest (1,642m) and oldest (25M years) lake. Contains 20% of surface freshwater.
items.details: Area: 31,722 km² | Depth: 1,642m | Volume: 23,615 km³ | Terrain: Rift lake, surrounding taiga and mountains | Attractions: Ice formations, Olkhon Island, Great Baikal Trail | 2,500+ endemic species including Baikal seal (nerpa) | UNESCO Heritage

items.name: Lake Superior
items.description: Largest freshwater lake by surface area (82,100 km²).
items.details: Area: 82,100 km² | Max depth: 406m | Contains 10% of world's surface freshwater | Terrain: Rocky shores, boreal forest, sand dunes | Attractions: Pictured Rocks, Isle Royale, Apostle Islands | Shipwreck capital of the Great Lakes | Water cold enough to preserve wrecks

items.name: Lake Victoria
items.description: Africa's largest lake. Source of the White Nile.
items.details: Area: 68,800 km² | Max depth: 84m (shallow) | Shared by Tanzania, Uganda, Kenya | Terrain: Tropical shores, papyrus swamps, rocky islands | Attractions: Ssese Islands, fishing villages, source of the Nile | 200+ cichlid species (many endemic) | Nile perch fishery

items.name: Lake Titicaca
items.description: Highest navigable lake in the world (3,812m elevation).
items.details: Area: 8,372 km² | Max depth: 281m | Elevation: 3,812m | Terrain: High Altiplano, terraced shores, sacred islands | Attractions: Uros floating reed islands, Taquile, Isla del Sol | Peru-Bolivia border | Ancient Tiwanaku civilization | Deepest lake in South America

items.name: Dead Sea
items.description: Lowest point on Earth's surface (-430m). 10x saltier than ocean.
items.details: Area: 605 km² (shrinking) | Depth: 304m | Elevation: -430m | Salinity: 34.2% | Terrain: Salt flats, mineral deposits, desert shores | Attractions: Floating experience, Ein Gedi oasis, Masada | Shrinking 1m/year | Jordan-Israel border | Therapeutic mud

items.name: Lake Tanganyika
items.description: 2nd deepest lake (1,470m). World's longest freshwater lake.
items.details: Area: 32,900 km² | Depth: 1,470m | Length: 676 km | Terrain: Steep rift escarpments, sandy beaches | Attractions: Mahale Mountains (chimps), Gombe Stream NP | 4 bordering countries | 350+ cichlid species | East African Rift lake

items.name: Great Slave Lake
items.description: North America's deepest lake (614m).
items.details: Area: 27,200 km² | Depth: 614m | Terrain: Sub-arctic boreal, rocky Canadian Shield, ice roads | Attractions: Aurora borealis viewing, ice fishing, Yellowknife | Ice-covered 8 months/year | Ice road used for diamond mine supply

items.name: Lake Malawi
items.description: African Great Lake. Most fish species of any lake on Earth.
items.details: Area: 29,600 km² | Depth: 706m | Terrain: Tropical rift lake, sandy beaches, rocky coves | Attractions: Cape Maclear, snorkeling with cichlids, Likoma Island | 700+ cichlid species | East African Rift | UNESCO Heritage | 'Lake of Stars'

items.name: Sahara Desert
items.description: World's largest hot desert (9.2M km²). Nearly the size of the US.
items.details: Area: 9.2M km² across 11 countries | Terrain: Sand dunes (ergs), rocky plateaus (hamadas), gravel plains (regs), oases | Attractions: Erg Chebbi dunes, Tassili n'Ajjer rock art, Siwa Oasis, Saharan eye (Richat Structure) | Temperatures reach 58°C | Was green 6,000 years ago

items.name: Antarctic Desert
items.description: Earth's largest desert by area (14.2M km²). A cold desert.
items.details: Area: 14.2M km² | Terrain: Ice sheets, nunataks, dry valleys, glaciers | Attractions: McMurdo Dry Valleys (most Mars-like place on Earth), South Pole | Average precipitation 166mm/year | Contains 70% of Earth's freshwater as ice | Coldest: -89.2°C

items.name: Arabian Desert
items.description: 2.3 million km². Includes the Rub' al Khali (Empty Quarter).
items.details: Area: 2.33M km² | Terrain: Sand seas, gravel plains, wadis, sabkhas (salt flats) | Attractions: Rub' al Khali (largest continuous sand desert, 650,000 km²), fossil rivers, oil fields | Oil reserves beneath sands | Extreme temperatures (-12°C to 56°C)

items.name: Gobi Desert
items.description: Asia's largest desert. Rain shadow of the Himalayas.
items.details: Area: 1.3M km² across Mongolia and China | Terrain: Rocky steppes, sand dunes, gravel plains, canyons | Attractions: Flaming Cliffs (dinosaur fossils), Yolyn Am gorge, Khongoryn Els dunes | Expanding southward 3,600 km²/year | Extreme temperatures (-40°C to 50°C)

items.name: Atacama Desert
items.description: Driest non-polar desert. Some areas haven't seen rain in centuries.
items.details: Area: 105,000 km² in Chile | Terrain: Salt flats, volcanic peaks, geysers, moon-like valleys | Attractions: Valle de la Luna, El Tatio geysers, ALMA observatory, flamingo salt lakes | NASA Mars analog site | Lithium mining boom | World's best stargazing

items.name: Kalahari Desert
items.description: Semi-arid savanna spanning Botswana, Namibia, South Africa.
items.details: Area: 900,000 km² | Terrain: Red sand dunes, scrubland, pans, Makgadikgadi salt flats | Attractions: Central Kalahari Game Reserve, Deception Valley, meerkats | San (Bushmen) homeland | Okavango Delta on northern edge | Wildlife: gemsbok, lions, springbok

items.name: Mariana Trench
items.description: Deepest point on Earth: Challenger Deep at 10,935m.
items.details: Length: 2,550 km | Width: 69 km | Depth: 10,935m | Terrain: Abyssal zone, hydrothermal vents, ultra-deep hadal zone | Pressure: 1,086 bar | Temperatures 1-4°C | Microbial life found at bottom | Only 3 crewed descents

items.name: Grand Canyon
items.description: 446 km long, up to 1,800m deep. 2 billion years of geological history.
items.details: Length: 446 km | Width: up to 29 km | Depth: 1,800m | Terrain: Layered sedimentary rock, desert mesas, Colorado River | Attractions: South Rim, Skywalk, Havasu Falls, rafting the Colorado | UNESCO Heritage | 40 rock layers visible | 5-6 million years of carving

items.name: Puerto Rico Trench
items.description: Deepest point in the Atlantic Ocean (8,376m).
items.details: Length: 800 km | Depth: 8,376m (Milwaukee Deep) | Terrain: Deep oceanic trench, tectonic boundary | Caribbean plate subducting under North American plate | Tsunami risk for Caribbean islands | Seismically very active

items.name: Tonga Trench
items.description: 2nd deepest trench (10,882m). Fastest tectonic subduction on Earth.
items.details: Length: 860 km | Depth: 10,882m (Horizon Deep) | Pacific plate subducting at 24 cm/year (fastest on Earth) | Terrain: Ultra-deep hadal zone | Hunga Tonga volcanic eruption 2022 created massive atmospheric shockwave

items.name: Fish River Canyon
items.description: 2nd largest canyon in the world. 160 km long, 27 km wide.
items.details: Length: 160 km | Width: 27 km | Depth: 550m | Area: Namibia | Terrain: Arid rocky gorge, seasonal river, hot springs | Attractions: 5-day hiking trail, Ai-Ais hot springs, viewpoints | Formed by erosion and tectonic activity | 500 million years old

items.name: Greenland
items.description: World's largest island (2.17M km²). Ice sheet contains 7m of sea level rise.
items.details: Area: 2.17M km² | 80% ice-covered | Terrain: Ice sheet, fjords, tundra, nunataks | Attractions: Ilulissat Icefjord (UNESCO), Northern Lights, Disko Bay | Rare earth minerals beneath ice | Climate sentinel — ice loss accelerating

items.name: Borneo
items.description: 3rd largest island. Shared by Malaysia, Indonesia, Brunei.
items.details: Area: 743,330 km² | Terrain: World's oldest rainforest (130M years), mountains, mangroves, cave systems | Attractions: Mount Kinabalu (4,095m), Mulu Caves, Danum Valley, orangutan sanctuaries | Rapidly deforesting for palm oil | 15,000 plant species

items.name: Madagascar
items.description: 4th largest island. Split from India 88M years ago. 90% endemic species.
items.details: Area: 592,800 km² | Terrain: Rainforest, dry deciduous forest, spiny forest, highlands | Attractions: Avenue of the Baobabs, Tsingy de Bemaraha, lemurs, chameleons | 90% of wildlife found nowhere else | Rapidly deforesting | Unique baobab species

items.name: Hawaiian Islands
items.description: Volcanic hotspot chain in the Pacific. Most isolated population center on Earth.
items.details: Area: 28,311 km² | Terrain: Shield volcanoes, tropical beaches, lava fields, rainforest | Attractions: Mauna Kea observatory, Na Pali Coast, Haleakala, active lava flows | Moving NW at 7 cm/year over hotspot | Mauna Kea 10,210m from ocean floor

items.name: Galápagos Islands
items.description: Volcanic archipelago. Darwin's laboratory of evolution.
items.details: Area: 7,880 km² | 19 islands | Terrain: Volcanic lava fields, beaches, highlands, marine environments | Attractions: Giant tortoises, marine iguanas, blue-footed boobies, snorkeling with sea lions | Nazca plate hotspot | UNESCO Heritage | 97% national park

items.name: Iceland
items.description: Island on the Mid-Atlantic Ridge. Geothermal paradise.
items.details: Area: 103,000 km² | Terrain: Volcanoes, glaciers, geysers, lava fields, fjords | Attractions: Golden Circle, Blue Lagoon, Jökulsárlón glacier lagoon, Northern Lights | 30+ active volcanic systems | 11% glaciated | 100% renewable electricity

items.name: Japan Archipelago
items.description: 6,852 islands at the junction of four tectonic plates.
items.details: Area: 377,975 km² | Terrain: Volcanic mountains, forests, coastal plains, hot springs | Attractions: Mount Fuji, Yakushima ancient cedars, Okinawa reefs, onsen towns | Most seismically active inhabited region | 111 active volcanoes | 10% of world's active volcanoes

items.name: New Zealand
items.description: Zealandia microcontinent fragment. Alpine Fault bisects South Island.
items.details: Area: 268,021 km² | Terrain: Fiords, volcanic plateau, glaciers, rainforest, geothermal fields | Attractions: Milford Sound, Tongariro Alpine Crossing, Rotorua geysers, Franz Josef Glacier | Southern Alps rising 7mm/year | Unique kiwi, tuatara

items.name: Svalbard
items.description: Arctic archipelago between Norway and North Pole.
items.details: Area: 61,022 km² | Terrain: Glaciers (60% ice-covered), tundra, fjords, permafrost | Attractions: Global Seed Vault, Northern Lights, polar bear safaris, ice caves | More polar bears than humans | Northernmost permanent settlement (Longyearbyen)

items.name: Indonesian Archipelago
items.description: World's largest archipelago: 17,508 islands spanning 5,120 km.
items.details: Area: 1.9M km² (land) | 17,508 islands | Terrain: Volcanoes, rainforest, coral reefs, mangroves | Attractions: Komodo dragons, Bali temples, Raja Ampat diving, Borobudur | Ring of Fire: 130 active volcanoes | Wallace Line divides Asian/Australasian fauna

items.name: Arabian Peninsula
items.description: World's largest peninsula (3.24M km²). Sits on the Arabian Plate.
items.details: Area: 3.24M km² | Terrain: Desert (Rub' al Khali), mountain ranges (Hejaz, Hajar), coastal plains | Attractions: Petra, Empty Quarter, Socotra Island, Oman fjords | World's largest oil reserves | Separated from Africa by Red Sea rift | 7 countries

items.name: Indian Subcontinent
items.description: Tectonic plate that collided with Eurasia, creating the Himalayas.
items.details: Area: 4.4M km² | Terrain: Deccan Plateau (basalt traps), Indo-Gangetic plains, Western/Eastern Ghats, Thar Desert | Attractions: Western Ghats biodiversity hotspot, Deccan volcanism legacy, Sundarbans | Monsoon climate system | Continental collision ongoing

items.name: Scandinavian Peninsula
items.description: Largest peninsula in Europe. Glacially sculpted fjords.
items.details: Area: 750,000 km² | Length: 1,850 km | Terrain: Fjords, mountains, boreal forest, tundra, Arctic coast | Attractions: Norwegian Fjords (Geirangerfjord, Sognefjord), Lofoten, Northern Lights | Post-glacial rebound: rising 1 cm/year | Baltic Shield (oldest rock in Europe)

items.name: Iberian Peninsula
items.description: Southwestern Europe. Spain and Portugal. Mediterranean-Atlantic divide.
items.details: Area: 583,000 km² | Terrain: Central plateau (Meseta), mountain ranges (Pyrenees, Sierra Nevada), river valleys | Attractions: Pyrenees hiking, Algarve coast, Picos de Europa, volcanic Canary Islands | Strait of Gibraltar separates from Africa | Mediterranean climate

items.name: Korean Peninsula
items.description: 1,100 km peninsula between Sea of Japan and Yellow Sea.
items.details: Area: 220,847 km² | Length: 1,100 km | Terrain: Mountainous (70%), coastal plains, volcanic (Jeju) | Attractions: DMZ, Jeju volcanic island (UNESCO), Seoraksan NP | Tectonic stability | Monsoon climate | Hot springs from volcanic activity

items.name: Italian Peninsula
items.description: Boot-shaped. Apennine Mountains form the spine. Active volcanism.
items.details: Area: 131,337 km² | Length: 1,000 km | Terrain: Apennine Mountains, volcanic zones, limestone coasts, alluvial plains | Attractions: Amalfi Coast, Dolomites, Vesuvius, Etna, Cinque Terre | African plate subduction | Earthquake-prone | Thermal springs

items.name: Kamchatka Peninsula
items.description: Russia's volcanic frontier. 160 volcanoes, 29 active.
items.details: Area: 472,300 km² | Length: 1,250 km | Terrain: Active volcanoes, geysers, hot springs, tundra, bear country | Attractions: Valley of Geysers, Klyuchevskaya Sopka (highest active volcano in Eurasia), brown bear watching | UNESCO Heritage | Pacific Ring of Fire

items.name: Malay Peninsula
items.description: Connects mainland Asia to Maritime Southeast Asia.
items.details: Area: 233,000 km² | Length: 1,127 km | Terrain: Tropical rainforest, mangroves, limestone karst, tin-bearing alluvium | Attractions: Langkawi, Cameron Highlands, Taman Negara (130M year old rainforest) | Strait of Malacca (busiest shipping lane)

items.name: Sinai Peninsula
items.description: Triangular peninsula connecting Africa to Asia.
items.details: Area: 60,000 km² | Terrain: Granite mountains, desert wadis, coral coasts, sand dunes | Attractions: Mount Sinai, St. Catherine's Monastery, Dahab diving, Colored Canyon | Red Sea Rift on west | Land bridge between continents | Suez Canal on western edge

items.name: Balkans Peninsula
items.description: Southeastern European peninsula. Complex tectonic history.
items.details: Area: 470,000 km² | Terrain: Dinaric Alps, karst landscapes, river gorges, Mediterranean/Adriatic coast | Attractions: Plitvice Lakes, Bay of Kotor, Meteora, Albanian Riviera | Seismic activity | Adriatic and Aegean coastlines | Rich in caves and sinkholes

items.name: Antarctic Peninsula
items.description: Fastest-warming region on Earth. Extension of the Andes.
items.details: Area: 522,000 km² | Length: 1,300 km | Terrain: Ice shelves, glaciers, volcanic islands, sub-Antarctic tundra | Attractions: Deception Island (volcanic hot springs), Lemaire Channel, penguin colonies | Warming 3°C in 50 years | Larsen ice shelf collapses

items.name: Africa
items.description: 2nd largest continent. Oldest continental crust. Splitting along East African Rift.
items.details: Area: 30.37M km² | 54 countries | Terrain: Sahara Desert, tropical rainforest, Great Rift Valley, savannas, mountains | Attractions: Victoria Falls, Serengeti, Sahara, Kilimanjaro, Nile River | Oldest human fossils | Splitting into two plates along East African Rift

items.name: Antarctica
items.description: 5th largest continent. 98% ice-covered. Contains 70% of Earth's freshwater.
items.details: Area: 14.2M km² | Terrain: Ice sheets (up to 4.8 km thick), nunataks, dry valleys, volcanic islands | Attractions: South Pole, McMurdo Dry Valleys, penguin rookeries, icebergs | No permanent population | Coldest recorded: -89.2°C | Ozone hole | Governed by Antarctic Treaty

items.name: Asia
items.description: Largest continent. 44.58M km². Most diverse geology on Earth.
items.details: Area: 44.58M km² | Terrain: Highest (Everest 8,849m) and lowest (Dead Sea -430m) points, steppes, tundra, tropics, deserts | Attractions: Himalayas, Gobi Desert, Maldives, Kamchatka geysers, Baikal | Most diverse geology and climate zones | 4.7 billion people

items.name: Europe
items.description: 6th largest continent. Geologically, western peninsula of Eurasia.
items.details: Area: 10.18M km² | Terrain: Alps, Scandinavian fjords, volcanic Mediterranean, ancient shields, karst landscapes | Attractions: Norwegian Fjords, Dolomites, Iceland, Santorini, Plitvice Lakes | Ice age legacy shaped landscape | Most coastline-to-area ratio

items.name: North America
items.description: 3rd largest continent. From Arctic to tropics.
items.details: Area: 24.71M km² | Terrain: Rocky Mountains, Great Plains, Canadian Shield, deserts, tropical forests | Attractions: Grand Canyon, Yellowstone, Niagara Falls, Hawaiian volcanoes, Banff | Diverse geology from 4-billion-year-old shield to active volcanoes

items.name: South America
items.description: 4th largest continent. Andes, Amazon, and Atacama.
items.details: Area: 17.84M km² | Terrain: Andes (longest range), Amazon Basin (largest river), Atacama (driest desert), Patagonian ice | Attractions: Angel Falls, Iguazu Falls, Galápagos, Salar de Uyuni, Perito Moreno | Amazon: 20% of world's oxygen and freshwater

items.name: Oceania
items.description: Smallest continental region. Australia + Pacific Islands.
items.details: Area: 8.53M km² | Terrain: Australian outback, Great Barrier Reef, volcanic Pacific islands, New Zealand Alps | Attractions: Uluru, Great Barrier Reef, Milford Sound, Bora Bora, Fiji | Ring of Fire volcanic islands | Great Artesian Basin (world's largest underground aquifer)

items.name: Suez Canal (Geological)
items.description: 163 km artificial waterway connecting Mediterranean and Red Sea.
items.details: Length: 193.3 km | Width: 205-225m | Terrain: Cut through sandy Isthmus of Suez, no locks (sea-level canal) | Attractions: Port Said, Ismailia, canal viewing points | ~12% of global trade passes through | Widened and deepened in 2015 | 19,000+ ships/year

items.name: Panama Canal (Geological)
items.description: 82 km canal across the Isthmus of Panama. Uses locks to raise ships 26m.
items.details: Length: 82 km | Terrain: Culebra Cut through Continental Divide, Gatun Lake (artificial), lock chambers | Attractions: Miraflores Locks visitor center, Gatun Lake | 3 sets of locks | Expanded in 2016 for Neo-Panamax ships | Drought threatens capacity

items.name: Kiel Canal
items.description: 98 km canal connecting North Sea and Baltic Sea across Germany.
items.details: Length: 98 km | Width: 162m | Terrain: Flat North German Plain, no locks needed | World's busiest artificial waterway by vessel count (30,000+/year) | Saves 460 km vs going around Denmark | Built 1887-1895

items.name: Corinth Canal
items.description: 6.4 km canal cutting through the Isthmus of Corinth, Greece.
items.details: Length: 6.4 km | Depth: 8m | Terrain: Sheer limestone walls up to 80m high, cut through solid rock | Attractions: Bungee jumping from bridge, boat tours | Too narrow (24.6m) for modern ships | Dreamed of since 7th century BC, built 1881-1893

items.name: Antarctic Ice Sheet
items.description: Largest single mass of ice on Earth. 26.5 million km³.
items.details: Area: 14M km² | Volume: 26.5M km³ | Thickness: up to 4,800m | Terrain: Vast ice plateau, ice streams, ice shelves, nunataks | If melted: 58m sea level rise | East sheet relatively stable; West Antarctic Ice Sheet vulnerable to marine collapse | Contains 70% of Earth's freshwater

items.name: Greenland Ice Sheet
items.description: 2nd largest ice body. Contains 7.2m of potential sea level rise.
items.details: Area: 1.71M km² | Volume: 2.85M km³ | Thickness: up to 3,000m | Terrain: Ice dome, outlet glaciers, meltwater lakes, crevasse fields | Losing 280 billion tonnes/year | Ice cores reveal 100,000+ years of climate history | Ilulissat Icefjord (UNESCO)

items.name: Vatnajökull
items.description: Europe's largest glacier by volume. Covers active volcanoes.
items.details: Area: 7,900 km² | Thickness: up to 1,000m | Terrain: Ice cap covering volcanoes, glacial rivers, ice caves, outlet glaciers | Attractions: Crystal ice caves, Jökulsárlón glacier lagoon, Skaftafell | Subglacial eruptions cause jökulhlaups (glacial floods) | National park

items.name: Perito Moreno Glacier
items.description: One of few advancing glaciers. Spectacular calving events.
items.details: Area: 250 km² | Width: 5 km at terminus | Height: 74m above water | Terrain: Advancing glacier, calving face, glacial lake | Attractions: Walkways for viewing calving, ice trekking, boat tours | Southern Patagonian Ice Field | UNESCO Heritage | Advances and ruptures cyclically

items.name: Siachen Glacier
items.description: World's highest battleground. 2nd longest non-polar glacier.
items.details: Area: 700 km² | Length: 76 km | Terrain: High-altitude glacial terrain at 5,400m+, crevasses, ice walls | India-Pakistan military dispute since 1984 | Soldiers stationed at 6,000m+ elevation | Karakoram Range | Extremely harsh conditions (-60°C)

timelineEvents.name: Earth Forms
timelineEvents.description: Accretion from solar nebula. Molten surface. Moon-forming impact.

timelineEvents.name: First Oceans
timelineEvents.description: Water vapor condenses. First liquid oceans form on cooling crust.

timelineEvents.name: First Life
timelineEvents.description: Earliest microbial life in hydrothermal vents. Stromatolites.

timelineEvents.name: Great Oxidation Event
timelineEvents.description: Cyanobacteria fill atmosphere with oxygen. Mass extinction of anaerobes.

timelineEvents.name: Snowball Earth
timelineEvents.description: Entire planet frozen. Ice reaches equator. Lasted 57 million years.

timelineEvents.name: Cambrian Explosion
timelineEvents.description: Most major animal phyla appear in 20 million years. Eyes evolve.

timelineEvents.name: First Land Plants
timelineEvents.description: Mosses and liverworts colonize land. Transform Earth's surface.

timelineEvents.name: First Forests
timelineEvents.description: Trees evolve. Forests create soil, change atmosphere and climate.

timelineEvents.name: Pangaea Assembles
timelineEvents.description: All continents merge into supercontinent Pangaea.

timelineEvents.name: The Great Dying
timelineEvents.description: Largest mass extinction. Siberian Traps volcanism. 96% marine species die.

timelineEvents.name: First Dinosaurs
timelineEvents.description: Small bipedal reptiles evolve. Will dominate for 165 million years.

timelineEvents.name: Pangaea Splits
timelineEvents.description: Pangaea breaks into Laurasia (north) and Gondwana (south).

timelineEvents.name: Atlantic Ocean Opens
timelineEvents.description: Rift between Africa and North America widens into ocean.

timelineEvents.name: First Birds
timelineEvents.description: Archaeopteryx: feathered dinosaur. Flight evolves.

timelineEvents.name: India Breaks from Africa
timelineEvents.description: Indian plate separates and races north toward Asia.

timelineEvents.name: K-Pg Extinction
timelineEvents.description: Chicxulub asteroid. Non-avian dinosaurs extinct. Mammals rise.

timelineEvents.name: India Hits Asia
timelineEvents.description: Indian plate collides with Eurasian plate. Himalayas begin rising.

timelineEvents.name: Himalayas Form
timelineEvents.description: Collision continues. Himalayas rising. Alters global climate patterns.

timelineEvents.name: Alps Form
timelineEvents.description: African plate pushes into Europe. Alpine orogeny.

timelineEvents.name: Andes Rising
timelineEvents.description: Nazca plate subduction intensifies. Andes grow rapidly.

timelineEvents.name: East African Rift Opens
timelineEvents.description: Africa begins splitting. Rift valley, volcanoes, great lakes form.

timelineEvents.name: Mediterranean Dries Up
timelineEvents.description: Messinian Salinity Crisis: Mediterranean evaporates almost completely.

timelineEvents.name: Panama Seaway Closes
timelineEvents.description: Isthmus of Panama connects Americas. Alters ocean currents. Ice ages begin.

timelineEvents.name: Ice Ages Begin
timelineEvents.description: Pleistocene glaciation cycles. Ice sheets advance and retreat.

timelineEvents.name: Last Glacial Maximum
timelineEvents.description: Ice sheets 3km thick over Canada and Scandinavia. Sea level 120m lower.

timelineEvents.name: Holocene Begins
timelineEvents.description: Current warm period. Glaciers retreat. Human civilization flourishes.

timelineEvents.name: Present Day
timelineEvents.description: Current continental arrangement. Continents continue to drift.

timelineEvents.name: Last Ice Age
timelineEvents.description: Sea levels 120m lower. Land bridges connect continents. Massive ice sheets cover North America and Europe.

timelineEvents.name: Late Cenozoic
timelineEvents.description: Himalayas rising rapidly. Americas connect via Panama. Mediterranean dries up (Messinian Crisis).

timelineEvents.name: Early Cenozoic
timelineEvents.description: India collides with Asia. Australia separates from Antarctica. Atlantic widening.

timelineEvents.name: Late Cretaceous
timelineEvents.description: Asteroid strikes Chicxulub. Dinosaurs go extinct. Continents recognizable but still moving.

timelineEvents.name: Jurassic
timelineEvents.description: Pangaea breaking apart. Atlantic Ocean opening. Age of dinosaurs.

timelineEvents.name: Triassic / Pangaea
timelineEvents.description: All continents united as Pangaea. Single ocean Panthalassa. Life recovering from Great Dying.

timelineEvents.name: Permian Extinction
timelineEvents.description: The Great Dying: 96% of marine species and 70% of land species go extinct.

timelineEvents.name: Carboniferous
timelineEvents.description: Vast coal swamp forests. Oxygen at 35%. Giant insects. Pangaea assembling.

timelineEvents.name: Ordovician
timelineEvents.description: Life explosion in oceans. First land plants. Gondwana drifts over South Pole.

domains.title: Geopolitical Atlas
domains.subtitle: Explore chokepoints, conflict zones, alliances, and power dynamics across the globe

items.name: Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Controls ~20% of global oil transit. One of the world's most strategically vital chokepoints.
items.details: Between Iran and Oman/UAE. ~21 million barrels of oil pass through daily.

items.name: Suez Canal
items.description: Handles ~12% of global trade. A single blockage in 2021 cost $9.6B per day.
items.details: 193 km long, connecting Mediterranean to Red Sea. Over 19,000 ships transit annually.

items.name: Strait of Malacca
items.description: Shortest sea route between Indian and Pacific Oceans. ~25% of all traded goods pass through.
items.details: 900 km long, narrows to 2.8 km at Phillips Channel. Critical for China, Japan, South Korea.

items.name: Panama Canal
items.description: Connects Atlantic and Pacific. Handles 5% of world trade. Expanded in 2016 for mega-ships.
items.details: 82 km long. Over 14,000 vessels transit annually. Drought threatens capacity.

items.name: Bab el-Mandeb
items.description: Gateway between Red Sea and Gulf of Aden. ~6.2 million barrels of oil daily.
items.details: Only 26 km wide. Yemen conflict and Houthi attacks have disrupted shipping.

items.name: Turkish Straits
items.description: Bosphorus and Dardanelles control Black Sea access. Critical for Russian naval power.
items.details: Governed by Montreux Convention (1936). Turkey controls warship transit.

items.name: Strait of Gibraltar
items.description: Only 14.3 km wide. Gateway between Atlantic and Mediterranean.
items.details: Over 100,000 ships per year. Separates Europe from Africa.

items.name: Danish Straits
items.description: Control access to the Baltic Sea. Critical for Northern European trade.
items.details: Three narrow passages between Denmark, Sweden, and Germany.

items.name: Kashmir
items.description: Disputed territory between India, Pakistan, and China since 1947.
items.details: Line of Control divides the region. Three wars fought over it. Nuclear-armed neighbors.

items.name: Eastern Ukraine
items.description: Active conflict zone since 2014. Full-scale invasion by Russia in 2022.
items.details: Donetsk and Luhansk regions. Europe's largest conflict since WWII.

items.name: Syria
items.description: Civil war since 2011 involving multiple state and non-state actors.
items.details: Russian, Turkish, Iranian, and US military presence. Millions displaced.

items.name: Yemen
items.description: Civil war since 2014. Saudi-led coalition vs Houthi rebels backed by Iran.
items.details: Worst humanitarian crisis. Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping since 2023.

items.name: Sudan
items.description: Civil conflict between military factions since April 2023.
items.details: SAF vs RSF. Millions displaced. Risk of state collapse.

items.name: Myanmar
items.description: Military coup in 2021 sparked civil war. Ethnic armed organizations resist junta.
items.details: Rohingya crisis. Multiple resistance movements. Strategic between India and China.

items.name: Gaza
items.description: One of the most contested and densely populated territories on Earth.
items.details: 365 km². Over 2 million people. Blockaded since 2007.

items.name: Sahel Region
items.description: Belt of instability across West Africa. Jihadist insurgencies and coups.
items.details: Mali, Burkina Faso, Niger. French withdrawal. Russian Wagner presence.

items.name: South China Sea
items.description: Disputed waters with $3.4 trillion in annual trade. Six-nation territorial claims.
items.details: China's Nine-Dash Line. Spratly and Paracel Islands. Artificial island building.

items.name: Taiwan Strait
items.description: Most sensitive flashpoint in global geopolitics. 90% of advanced chip manufacturing.
items.details: 180 km wide. TSMC produces majority of world's advanced semiconductors.

items.name: Crimea
items.description: Annexed by Russia in 2014. Internationally recognized as Ukrainian territory.
items.details: Strategic Black Sea naval base at Sevastopol. Kerch Bridge connects to Russia.

items.name: Golan Heights
items.description: Captured by Israel from Syria in 1967. Annexed in 1981.
items.details: Strategic high ground. Water resources. UN buffer zone.

items.name: Western Sahara
items.description: Africa's last colony. Disputed between Morocco and Polisario Front.
items.details: Rich in phosphates and fishing. Sand wall divides the territory.

items.name: Kuril Islands
items.description: Disputed between Russia and Japan since 1945. No peace treaty signed.
items.details: Four southernmost islands claimed by Japan. Rich fishing grounds.

items.name: Falkland Islands
items.description: British overseas territory claimed by Argentina. 1982 war.
items.details: Oil exploration potential. Strategic South Atlantic position.

items.name: Diego Garcia
items.description: US/UK military base in the Indian Ocean. Strategic power projection hub.
items.details: Bomber base. Naval support. Key to Middle East and Asia operations.

items.name: Ramstein Air Base
items.description: Largest US Air Force base outside the US. NATO command hub.
items.details: Germany. Coordinates Ukraine aid. Air operations center for Europe/Africa.

items.name: Djibouti
items.description: Only country hosting both US and Chinese military bases.
items.details: Guards Bab el-Mandeb. French, Japanese, Italian bases too.

items.name: Okinawa
items.description: Major US military presence in the Pacific. 32 military installations.
items.details: 70% of US facilities in Japan. Controversial local opposition.

items.name: Guam
items.description: US territory. Major Pacific military hub. 'Tip of the spear.'
items.details: Anderson Air Force Base. Naval Base Guam. Missile defense installations.

items.name: Shenzhen
items.description: China's tech manufacturing capital. $400B+ GDP.
items.details: Home to Huawei, Tencent, BYD, DJI. Special Economic Zone since 1980.

items.name: Singapore
items.description: Global shipping and financial hub. World's busiest transshipment port.
items.details: GDP per capita ~$65,000. Free trade agreements with 27 partners.

items.name: Dubai
items.description: Middle East's financial and logistics center. Jebel Ali: world's largest man-made port.
items.details: Free zones attract global business. Hub connecting Europe, Asia, Africa.

items.name: Rotterdam
items.description: Europe's largest port. Gateway to the EU market of 450 million.
items.details: Handles 470 million tonnes annually. Major energy hub.

items.name: Arctic Shipping Routes
items.description: Emerging trade routes as ice melts. Could cut Asia-Europe transit by 40%.
items.details: Northern Sea Route along Russia. Northwest Passage through Canada.

items.name: Washington D.C.
items.description: US: ~5,500 nuclear warheads. World's largest military budget ($886B).
items.details: NATO leader. Nuclear triad: ICBMs, SLBMs, strategic bombers.

items.name: Moscow
items.description: Russia: ~6,200 warheads. Largest nuclear arsenal globally.
items.details: Modernizing delivery systems. Sarmat ICBM. Poseidon torpedo.

items.name: Beijing
items.description: China: ~500 warheads, rapidly expanding. Projected 1,500 by 2035.
items.details: Building hundreds of new ICBM silos. Nuclear triad development.

items.name: Islamabad
items.description: Pakistan: ~170 warheads. Fastest growing nuclear arsenal.
items.details: Tactical nuclear weapons development. India-focused deterrence.

items.name: New Delhi
items.description: India: ~164 warheads. No-first-use policy. Nuclear triad.
items.details: Agni-V ICBM range 5,000+ km. Nuclear submarine fleet.

items.name: Pyongyang
items.description: North Korea: ~50 warheads estimated. ICBM capable.
items.details: Hwasong-17 ICBM. Claimed hydrogen bomb. Sanctions-resistant program.

items.name: Tel Aviv
items.description: Israel: Undeclared nuclear power. Estimated 90 warheads.
items.details: Policy of deliberate ambiguity. Dimona reactor. Jericho missiles.

items.name: Lagos
items.description: Nigeria: Africa's largest economy. Population to surpass 400M by 2050.
items.details: Oil producer. Tech startup hub. ECOWAS leader.

items.name: Jakarta
items.description: Indonesia: World's 4th most populous. Growing maritime power.
items.details: ASEAN leader. Nickel superpower for EV batteries. Moving capital to Nusantara.

items.name: Riyadh
items.description: Saudi Arabia: Vision 2030 transformation. OPEC+ leader.
items.details: NEOM megaproject. Sports diplomacy. Balancing US and China relations.

items.name: Brasília
items.description: Brazil: BRICS member. Amazon steward. Agricultural superpower.
items.details: World's largest tropical forest. Major soy, beef, iron ore exporter.

items.name: Ankara
items.description: Turkey: NATO member straddling Europe and Asia. Regional power broker.
items.details: Bayraktar drones changed modern warfare. Controls Turkish Straits.

timelineEvents.name: Egyptian Civilization
timelineEvents.description: One of the longest-lasting civilizations. Pharaohs, pyramids, and the Nile.

timelineEvents.name: Sumerian City-States
timelineEvents.description: First civilization. Invented writing, the wheel, and mathematics.

timelineEvents.name: Akkadian Empire
timelineEvents.description: Sargon of Akkad: first empire in history. United Mesopotamia.

timelineEvents.name: Babylonian Empire
timelineEvents.description: Hammurabi's Code. Hanging Gardens. Nebuchadnezzar II.

timelineEvents.name: Hittite Empire
timelineEvents.description: Anatolian iron-age superpower. Rivaled Egypt.

timelineEvents.name: Zhou Dynasty
timelineEvents.description: Longest Chinese dynasty. Mandate of Heaven concept.

timelineEvents.name: Assyrian Empire
timelineEvents.description: Brutal military empire. Library of Ashurbanipal.

timelineEvents.name: Achaemenid Persian Empire
timelineEvents.description: From Egypt to India. Cyrus the Great. Royal Road.

timelineEvents.name: Athenian Democracy
timelineEvents.description: Birthplace of democracy. Golden Age of Pericles.

timelineEvents.name: Roman Republic & Empire
timelineEvents.description: From city-state to greatest empire. Law, roads, architecture.

timelineEvents.name: Maurya Empire
timelineEvents.description: Unified India. Ashoka spread Buddhism.

timelineEvents.name: Han Dynasty
timelineEvents.description: Silk Road. Paper invention. Chinese golden age.

timelineEvents.name: Gupta Empire
timelineEvents.description: India's Golden Age. Mathematics (zero), astronomy, art.

timelineEvents.name: Sasanian Empire
timelineEvents.description: Last great Persian empire before Islam. Rival of Rome.

timelineEvents.name: Byzantine Empire
timelineEvents.description: Eastern Roman Empire. Constantinople. Preserved Greek knowledge.

timelineEvents.name: Umayyad Caliphate
timelineEvents.description: Largest Islamic empire. Spain to Central Asia.

timelineEvents.name: Abbasid Caliphate
timelineEvents.description: Islamic Golden Age. Baghdad: world's greatest city.

timelineEvents.name: Carolingian Empire
timelineEvents.description: Charlemagne unifies Western Europe. Holy Roman Empire precursor.

timelineEvents.name: Tang Dynasty
timelineEvents.description: China's cosmopolitan golden age. Poetry, art, Silk Road.

timelineEvents.name: Song Dynasty
timelineEvents.description: Technological peak: gunpowder, compass, printing, paper money.

timelineEvents.name: Mongol Empire
timelineEvents.description: Largest contiguous land empire. Genghis Khan to Kublai Khan.

timelineEvents.name: Mali Empire
timelineEvents.description: West African gold empire. Mansa Musa: richest ever.

timelineEvents.name: Ottoman Empire
timelineEvents.description: 624 years. Three continents. Constantinople to Mecca.

timelineEvents.name: Aztec Empire
timelineEvents.description: Mesoamerican empire. Tenochtitlan: population 200,000.

timelineEvents.name: Inca Empire
timelineEvents.description: Largest pre-Columbian empire. 4,000 km of Andes.

timelineEvents.name: Spanish Empire
timelineEvents.description: First global empire. Americas, Philippines, Netherlands.

timelineEvents.name: Portuguese Empire
timelineEvents.description: First and longest European colonial empire. Brazil, Africa, Asia.

timelineEvents.name: British Empire
timelineEvents.description: Largest empire in history. 'Sun never sets.' 35.5M km².

timelineEvents.name: Mughal Empire
timelineEvents.description: Indo-Islamic empire. Taj Mahal. 150M population.

timelineEvents.name: Qing Dynasty
timelineEvents.description: Last Chinese dynasty. Largest territory. Opium Wars.

timelineEvents.name: Russian Empire
timelineEvents.description: Largest contiguous empire. Peter the Great to Nicholas II.

timelineEvents.name: French Colonial Empire
timelineEvents.description: 2nd largest colonial empire. Africa, Southeast Asia, Caribbean.

timelineEvents.name: German Empire
timelineEvents.description: Bismarck's unification. Industrial powerhouse. WWI.

timelineEvents.name: Austro-Hungarian Empire
timelineEvents.description: Dual monarchy. Multi-ethnic empire. Dissolved after WWI.

timelineEvents.name: Soviet Union
timelineEvents.description: Communist superpower. Space race. Cold War.

timelineEvents.name: Nazi Germany
timelineEvents.description: Third Reich. WWII aggressor. Holocaust.

timelineEvents.name: NATO Alliance
timelineEvents.description: Western military alliance. 32 member nations.

timelineEvents.name: European Union
timelineEvents.description: 27-nation economic and political union. Single market.

timelineEvents.name: People's Republic of China
timelineEvents.description: World's 2nd largest economy. 1.4 billion people.

timelineEvents.name: BRICS
timelineEvents.description: Emerging powers bloc. 45% of world population.

timelineEvents.name: United Nations
timelineEvents.description: 193 member states. International peacekeeping and cooperation.

countryProfiles.name: United States of America
countryProfiles.description: 🇺🇸 GDP: $25.5T | Pop: 335M | Languages: English | Memberships: G7, G20, NATO, OECD, Five Eyes, UN Security Council (P5) | World's largest economy and military spender ($886B). 750+ overseas military bases in 80 countries. Dollar is global reserve currency.

countryProfiles.name: China
countryProfiles.description: 🇨🇳 GDP: $17.9T | Pop: 1.41B | Languages: Mandarin, Cantonese, regional | Memberships: G20, BRICS, SCO, UN Security Council (P5) | World's 2nd largest economy. Belt and Road Initiative spans 150+ countries. Fastest military modernization. Manufacturing superpower.

countryProfiles.name: Russia
