domains.title: Historical Atlas
domains.subtitle: Empires, wars, discoveries, and revolutions from 5000 BC to today

items.name: Sumerian Civilization
items.description: World's first civilization. Invented writing (cuneiform), the wheel, and the 60-minute hour.
items.details: Mesopotamia (modern Iraq). City-states: Ur, Uruk, Eridu. Ziggurats as religious centers. First written laws. Irrigation agriculture between Tigris and Euphrates.

items.name: Ancient Egypt Unification
items.description: Upper and Lower Egypt unified under Pharaoh Narmer. 3,000-year civilization begins.
items.details: Nile River civilization. Pyramids of Giza built ~2560 BC. Hieroglyphic writing. Mummification. Built one of the most enduring empires in history. Great Sphinx, Valley of the Kings.

items.name: Indus Valley Civilization
items.description: One of the earliest urban civilizations. Remarkable city planning with drainage systems.
items.details: Harappa and Mohenjo-daro. Population ~5 million. Advanced weights and measures. Undeciphered script. Declined ~1900 BC, possibly due to climate change.

items.name: Ancient China – Xia Dynasty
items.description: Legendary first dynasty of China. Beginning of Chinese dynastic civilization.
items.details: Yellow River valley. Bronze Age culture. Yu the Great and flood control. Followed by Shang Dynasty (~1600 BC) with oracle bones and bronze casting.

items.name: Stonehenge Construction
items.description: Neolithic monument built over 1,500 years. Purpose still debated.
items.details: Salisbury Plain, England. Bluestones transported 240 km from Wales. Astronomical alignment. Part of larger ritual landscape. UNESCO Heritage.

items.name: Minoan Civilization
items.description: Europe's first advanced civilization on Crete. Palace of Knossos.
items.details: Sophisticated art, architecture, and trade networks. Linear A script (undeciphered). Bull-leaping rituals. Destroyed ~1450 BC, possibly by Thera eruption.

items.name: Invention of Bronze
items.description: Copper-tin alloy revolutionized tools, weapons, and civilization.
items.details: First developed in Mesopotamia and Anatolia. Enabled better agriculture, warfare, and trade. Bronze Age lasted until ~1200 BC when iron replaced it.

items.name: Phoenician Alphabet
items.description: First widely-used phonetic alphabet. Ancestor of Greek, Latin, Arabic, and Hebrew scripts.
items.details: 22 consonant letters. Phoenicia (modern Lebanon). Spread via Mediterranean trade networks. Revolutionary simplification from hieroglyphs and cuneiform.

items.name: Bronze Age Collapse
items.description: Sudden collapse of every major civilization in the Eastern Mediterranean.
items.details: Mycenaean Greece, Hittite Empire, New Kingdom Egypt, Ugarit all fell. Causes: Sea Peoples invasions, drought, earthquakes, trade disruption. Ushered in a 300-year Dark Age.

items.name: Mesopotamian Trade Routes
items.description: Earliest long-distance trade networks connecting Mesopotamia to Indus Valley.
items.details: Lapis lazuli from Afghanistan, copper from Oman, timber from Lebanon. Maritime trade via Persian Gulf. Standardized weights and measures enabled commerce.

items.name: Rise of Athens & Democracy
items.description: Athenian democracy established by Cleisthenes. Foundation of Western political thought.
items.details: Direct democracy for male citizens. Agora as political center. Pericles' Golden Age (~460–430 BC). Parthenon built. Philosophy: Socrates, Plato, Aristotle.

items.name: Persian Empire (Achaemenid)
items.description: Largest empire the world had yet seen. Cyrus the Great's rule from Egypt to India.
items.details: 5.5M km² at peak. 44% of world population. Royal Road postal system. Tolerance of conquered cultures. Persepolis capital. Defeated by Alexander 330 BC.

items.name: Alexander the Great's Conquests
items.description: Conquered from Greece to India in 11 years. Spread Hellenistic culture across 5.2M km².
items.details: Macedon to Egypt to Persia to India. Founded Alexandria. Died at 32 in Babylon. Empire split into Ptolemaic, Seleucid, and Antigonid kingdoms. Greek became lingua franca.

items.name: Roman Republic Founded
items.description: Overthrow of monarchy. Senate and consuls established Republican governance.
items.details: SPQR. Senate, consuls, tribunes. Punic Wars against Carthage. Civil wars: Caesar, Pompey, Crassus. Julius Caesar assassinated 44 BC. Republic fell to Empire 27 BC.

items.name: Roman Empire at Peak
items.description: Under Trajan, Rome controlled 5M km² from Britain to Mesopotamia. ~70M people.
items.details: Pax Romana (27 BC – 180 AD). Roads, aqueducts, Colosseum, Pantheon. Latin as lingua franca. Roman law basis for Western legal systems. Fell 476 AD.

items.name: Silk Road Established
items.description: Trade network connecting China to Rome. 6,500 km of overland routes.
items.details: Zhang Qian's missions opened the route. Silk, spices, gold, ideas, religions, diseases traveled. Samarkand, Kashgar as key stops. Operated for ~1,500 years.

items.name: Birth of Buddhism
items.description: Siddhartha Gautama attains enlightenment. Buddhism spreads across Asia.
items.details: Bodh Gaya, India. Four Noble Truths. Eightfold Path. Spread to Sri Lanka, China, Japan, SE Asia. Ashoka's patronage (3rd century BC) crucial for expansion.

items.name: Qin Dynasty – China Unified
items.description: First Emperor Qin Shi Huang unifies China. Great Wall begun, terracotta army built.
items.details: Standardized writing, weights, measures, currency. Legalism philosophy. Terracotta Army (8,000 warriors). Burned books, buried scholars. Short dynasty (15 years) but transformative.

items.name: Fall of Rome
items.description: Western Roman Empire falls to Germanic king Odoacer. End of ancient world.
items.details: Decades of decline: inflation, military overspending, Gothic invasions. Visigoths sacked Rome 410. Vandals 455. Eastern (Byzantine) Empire survived until 1453.

items.name: Han Dynasty Golden Age
items.description: Han Dynasty rules China for 400 years. Paper invented, Silk Road flourishes.
items.details: Population ~60 million. Civil service exams. Confucianism as state philosophy. Paper invention ~105 AD. Seismograph. Acupuncture. 'Han Chinese' identity.

items.name: Carthage & Punic Wars
items.description: Three wars between Rome and Carthage. Hannibal crosses Alps with elephants.
items.details: 1st Punic War: naval supremacy. 2nd: Hannibal's invasion of Italy (218-201 BC). 3rd: Rome destroys Carthage (146 BC), salts the earth. Rome becomes Mediterranean superpower.

items.name: Maurya Empire
items.description: First empire to unite most of the Indian subcontinent under Chandragupta Maurya.
items.details: 5 million km². Ashoka the Great (268-232 BC) converted to Buddhism. Edicts of Ashoka. Arthashastra (political treatise). Pataliputra capital. Population ~50 million.

items.name: Crucifixion of Jesus
items.description: Founding event of Christianity, which would become the world's largest religion.
items.details: Jerusalem, Roman Judea. Spread by apostles, especially Paul. Legalized 313 AD (Edict of Milan). State religion 380 AD. Shaped Western civilization profoundly.

items.name: Rise of Islam
items.description: Prophet Muhammad's Hijra to Medina. Islam rapidly expands across Middle East and North Africa.
items.details: Mecca and Medina. Quran revelation. Rashidun Caliphate conquers Persia, Egypt, Levant within 30 years. Umayyad Caliphate spans from Spain to Central Asia by 750.

items.name: Byzantine Empire Peak
items.description: Under Justinian I, Byzantium reconquered Mediterranean. Hagia Sophia built.
items.details: Constantinople: world's largest city. Justinian's Code (Roman law). Hagia Sophia (537). Preserved Greek/Roman knowledge. Fell to Ottomans 1453.

items.name: Viking Age
items.description: Norse raiders, traders, and explorers reach from Baghdad to North America.
items.details: Lindisfarne 793. Settled Iceland, Greenland, Vinland (Newfoundland). Varangian Guard in Constantinople. Founded Normandy, Dublin, Kyiv. Advanced shipbuilding.

items.name: Mongol Empire
items.description: Genghis Khan unites Mongol tribes. Builds largest contiguous land empire ever (24M km²).
items.details: From Korea to Hungary. Estimated 40M killed. Pax Mongolica enabled Silk Road trade. Religious tolerance. Postal system (Yam). Grandson Kublai Khan ruled China.

items.name: Black Death
items.description: Bubonic plague kills 30–60% of Europe's population (75–200 million dead).
items.details: Arrived via Genoese ships from Crimea. Yersinia pestis bacterium. Destroyed feudal system. Labor shortages raised wages. Recurred for centuries. Changed European society forever.

items.name: Crusades Begin
items.description: 200 years of religious wars for control of the Holy Land.
items.details: 9 major Crusades (1096–1291). Jerusalem captured 1099. Saladin recaptured 1187. Cultural exchange between East and West. Military orders: Templars, Hospitallers.

items.name: Magna Carta
items.description: English barons force King John to accept limits on royal power. Foundation of rule of law.
items.details: Runnymede, England. No taxation without consent. Due process. Influenced US Constitution, UN Declaration of Human Rights. Foundation of constitutional governance.

items.name: Ottoman Empire Founded
items.description: Osman I founds dynasty that would rule for 624 years and control three continents.
items.details: Conquered Constantinople 1453 (Mehmed II). Suleiman the Magnificent (1520-1566). Controlled SE Europe, Middle East, North Africa. Fell after WWI (1922).

items.name: Mali Empire & Mansa Musa
items.description: Mansa Musa's pilgrimage to Mecca. Richest person in history (adjusted ~$400B).
items.details: West Africa. Gold and salt trade. Timbuktu university and mosques. Musa's hajj (1324) caused gold price crash in Egypt. Djenne-Djenno mosque.

items.name: Gutenberg Printing Press
items.description: Movable type printing revolutionizes knowledge distribution. Triggers information revolution.
items.details: Mainz, Germany. Gutenberg Bible (1455). Books became 80% cheaper. Literacy exploded. Enabled Reformation, Scientific Revolution, Enlightenment. Changed the world more than almost any invention.

items.name: Fall of Constantinople
items.description: Ottoman Sultan Mehmed II conquers the last remnant of the Roman Empire.
items.details: 53-day siege. Massive cannon breached Theodosian walls. End of Byzantine Empire (1,100 years). Greek scholars fled to Italy, sparking Renaissance. Istanbul becomes Ottoman capital.

items.name: Aztec Empire
items.description: Tenochtitlan founded on Lake Texcoco. Population reaches 200,000+.
items.details: Chinampas (floating gardens). Templo Mayor pyramid. Controlled central Mexico. Human sacrifice. Conquered by Cortés 1521 with 500 men plus native allies and smallpox.

items.name: Inca Empire
items.description: Largest empire in pre-Columbian Americas. 12M people across 4,000 km of Andes.
items.details: Cusco capital. Machu Picchu. 40,000 km road network. Quipu record-keeping. No writing, no wheel, no iron. Conquered by Pizarro 1532. Extraordinary engineering.

items.name: Great Zimbabwe
items.description: Largest stone structure in sub-Saharan Africa. Capital of a prosperous trading kingdom.
items.details: Zimbabwe. Stone walls without mortar. Gold, ivory, and Indian Ocean trade. Population ~18,000. Connected to Swahili coast trading networks. Declined ~1450.

items.name: Columbus Reaches Americas
items.description: Christopher Columbus lands in the Bahamas. Begins the Columbian Exchange.
items.details: Spanish-funded expedition. Connected Old and New Worlds permanently. Columbian Exchange: crops, animals, diseases. 90% of indigenous Americans died from European diseases.

items.name: Protestant Reformation
items.description: Martin Luther's 95 Theses split Western Christianity. Reshapes European politics.
items.details: Wittenberg, Germany. Challenged papal authority and indulgences. Printing press spread his ideas. Led to 30 Years' War, English Reformation, Calvanism. Permanent Catholic-Protestant divide.

items.name: Mughal Empire
items.description: Babur conquers Delhi. Mughal dynasty rules India for 330 years.
items.details: Peak under Akbar, Shah Jahan, Aurangzeb. 150M people (25% of world). Taj Mahal (1653). Persian-influenced art and architecture. Declined 1700s, ended by British.

items.name: Spanish Conquest of the Americas
items.description: Cortés conquers Aztec Empire. Pizarro conquers Incas. Spain dominates the New World.
items.details: Tenochtitlan fell 1521, Cusco 1533. Smallpox killed millions before soldiers arrived. Silver from Potosí fueled Spanish Empire. Encomienda system. Colonial rule lasted 300 years.

items.name: Atlantic Slave Trade Peak
items.description: 12.5 million Africans forcibly transported across the Atlantic (1500–1867).
items.details: Triangular trade: Europe → Africa → Americas. Sugar, cotton, tobacco plantations. Middle Passage: 15% mortality. Abolished: British 1807, US 1865. Lasting demographic and cultural impact.

items.name: Scientific Revolution
items.description: Copernicus publishes heliocentric model. Galileo, Newton follow. Modern science born.
items.details: Copernicus (1543), Galileo (1610 telescope), Kepler (planetary motion), Newton (1687 Principia). Scientific method. Royal Society (1660). Transformed understanding of universe.

items.name: American Revolution
items.description: 13 colonies revolt against Britain. Declaration of Independence (1776). Republic founded.
items.details: Boston Tea Party (1773). 'No taxation without representation.' George Washington. Constitution (1787). Bill of Rights. Inspired French Revolution and global independence movements.

items.name: French Revolution
items.description: Storming of the Bastille. Monarchy overthrown. Liberté, égalité, fraternité.
items.details: Reign of Terror (1793-94). Guillotine. Napoleon rises from chaos. Napoleonic Code. Spread revolutionary ideals across Europe. Ended feudalism. Declaration of Rights of Man.

items.name: Magellan's Circumnavigation
items.description: First voyage around the world proves Earth's circumference. Magellan dies en route.
items.details: 5 ships, 270 men departed Spain. 1 ship, 18 men returned (1522). Strait of Magellan. Crossed Pacific (named by Magellan). Magellan killed in Philippines. Juan Sebastián Elcano completed the voyage.

items.name: Qing Dynasty Established
items.description: Manchu conquest of China. Last imperial dynasty rules until 1912.
items.details: Kangxi, Qianlong emperors. Largest Chinese territory ever (13M km²). Population tripled to 400M. Isolationist policies. Opium Wars humiliation. Fell in Xinhai Revolution.

items.name: Industrial Revolution Begins
items.description: Steam engine, factories, and mechanization transform Britain and the world.
items.details: Manchester, England. Steam engine (Watt). Cotton mills. Railways. Urbanization. Child labor. Coal mining. Spread to Europe, US. Created modern capitalism and the working class.

items.name: Napoleonic Wars
items.description: Napoleon's campaigns reshape Europe's borders and spread revolutionary ideals.
items.details: France vs coalitions of European powers. Napoleon conquered most of Europe. Battle of Austerlitz (1805). Invasion of Russia (1812) — disastrous retreat. Waterloo (1815). Exiled to St. Helena.

items.name: Congress of Vienna
items.description: European powers redraw the map after Napoleon. Balance of power established.
items.details: Metternich, Talleyrand, Castlereagh. Restored monarchies. Concert of Europe. Relative peace for 100 years (until 1914). Conservative reaction to revolutionary ideals.

items.name: Scramble for Africa
items.description: Berlin Conference divides Africa among European powers. 90% colonized by 1914.
items.details: Britain, France, Belgium, Germany, Portugal, Italy, Spain carved up Africa. Arbitrary borders splitting ethnic groups. Congo Free State atrocities. Colonial rule until 1960s independence wave.

items.name: World War I
items.description: The Great War. 20 million dead. Empires collapse. Modern warfare born.
items.details: Trigger: Archduke Franz Ferdinand assassination. Trench warfare. Poison gas. Tanks. 4 empires fall (Ottoman, Austro-Hungarian, Russian, German). Treaty of Versailles. League of Nations.

items.name: Russian Revolution
items.description: Bolsheviks overthrow Tsar. Soviet Union established. Communism becomes global force.
items.details: February: Tsar abdicates. October: Lenin seizes power. Russian Civil War (1917-22). Stalin's purges and industrialization. Cold War ideology. USSR until 1991.

items.name: World War II
items.description: Deadliest conflict in history. 70–85 million dead. Reshapes entire world order.
items.details: Hitler invades Poland. Holocaust (6M Jews). Pacific War (Pearl Harbor, Hiroshima). D-Day. Stalin's Eastern Front. UN founded. Cold War begins. Decolonization accelerates.

items.name: Hiroshima & Nagasaki
items.description: First nuclear weapons used in warfare. Ushers in the Atomic Age.
items.details: August 6 & 9, 1945. ~200,000 dead. Japan surrenders. Nuclear arms race begins. MAD doctrine. Non-Proliferation Treaty (1968). Nuclear shadow over all subsequent geopolitics.

items.name: Meiji Restoration
items.description: Japan transforms from feudal isolation to industrial world power in one generation.
items.details: Emperor restored from Tokugawa Shogunate. Rapid industrialization and modernization. Western-style constitution. Defeated Russia (1905). First non-Western industrial power.

items.name: US Civil War
items.description: 620,000 dead. Union preserved. Slavery abolished. America's deadliest war.
items.details: North vs South over slavery and states' rights. Gettysburg, Sherman's March. Lincoln's Emancipation Proclamation (1863). 13th Amendment. Reconstruction era. Legacy still contested.

items.name: Theory of Relativity
items.description: Einstein's E=mc² and spacetime theories revolutionize physics.
items.details: Special Relativity (1905), General Relativity (1915). Bern, Switzerland. Space and time are interlinked. Gravity bends spacetime. Led to nuclear energy, GPS, black hole theory.

items.name: Penicillin Discovery
items.description: Alexander Fleming discovers first antibiotic. Saves hundreds of millions of lives.
items.details: St. Mary's Hospital, London. Accidental mold contamination. Mass-produced by WWII. Began antibiotic era. Nobel Prize 1945. Now facing antibiotic resistance crisis.

items.name: United Nations Founded
items.description: 51 nations create the UN to prevent another world war. Headquarters in New York.
items.details: Security Council (5 permanent members with veto). General Assembly. Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1948). Peacekeeping missions. 193 member states today.

items.name: Cold War
items.description: US vs Soviet Union ideological struggle. Nuclear standoff. Proxy wars worldwide.
items.details: Iron Curtain. Berlin Wall (1961-89). Korean War. Vietnam War. Cuban Missile Crisis (1962). Space Race. Arms race. Ended with Soviet collapse 1991.

items.name: Indian Independence
items.description: British India partitioned into India and Pakistan. Largest mass migration in history.
items.details: Gandhi's nonviolent resistance. Partition: 10-20 million displaced, 1-2 million killed. Kashmir dispute begins. India becomes world's largest democracy.

items.name: Moon Landing
items.description: Apollo 11: 'One small step for man, one giant leap for mankind.' Neil Armstrong walks on the Moon.
items.details: Houston, Texas (Mission Control). 384,400 km journey. 400,000 NASA workers. Cold War space race victory. 12 humans walked on the Moon (1969-1972). Inspired generations.

items.name: Fall of Berlin Wall
items.description: Berlin Wall falls. Cold War ends. Germany reunified. Soviet bloc collapses.
items.details: November 9, 1989. East Germans flood through checkpoints. Germany reunified 1990. Soviet Union dissolved 1991. NATO expanded eastward. 'End of History' proclaimed.

items.name: European Union Founded
items.description: Maastricht Treaty creates the EU. 27 nations in a single market with free movement.
items.details: Brussels. Single market, Euro currency (2002), Schengen Area. 450 million people. Largest trade bloc. Nobel Peace Prize 2012. Brexit (2020) was first departure.

items.name: Internet Revolution
items.description: World Wide Web goes public. Information revolution transforms every aspect of human life.
items.details: Tim Berners-Lee (CERN). Silicon Valley. Dot-com boom. Social media. Smartphones. AI. 5.3 billion internet users (2024). Largest transformation since printing press.

items.name: September 11 Attacks
items.description: Al-Qaeda attacks kill 2,977. Triggers 'War on Terror,' reshapes global security.
items.details: World Trade Center, Pentagon, Flight 93. US invades Afghanistan (2001), Iraq (2003). Patriot Act. Global surveillance expansion. $8 trillion spent. Shaped 21st century geopolitics.

items.name: COVID-19 Pandemic
items.description: Global pandemic kills 7M+ officially (est. 15-25M). Largest disruption since WWII.
items.details: Wuhan, China origin. SARS-CoV-2. Lockdowns worldwide. mRNA vaccines developed in record time. Economic devastation. Remote work revolution. Supply chain crisis.

items.name: Russia Invades Ukraine
items.description: Largest European conflict since WWII. Reshapes global alliances and energy.
items.details: February 24, 2022. Failed capture of Kyiv. Attritional warfare. NATO expansion (Finland, Sweden). Energy crisis. Sanctions. 500,000+ casualties estimated.

items.name: African Decolonization
items.description: Year of Africa: 17 nations gain independence. Colonial era ends across the continent.
items.details: 1960 was pivotal, but decolonization spanned 1950s-1970s. Kwame Nkrumah (Ghana, 1957), Patrice Lumumba (Congo). OAU founded 1963. Legacy of arbitrary borders persists.

items.name: Chinese Economic Reform
items.description: Deng Xiaoping opens China to market economy. Lifts 800 million from poverty.
items.details: Special Economic Zones. 'Socialism with Chinese characteristics.' GDP grew 40x. World's factory. WTO member (2001). Now world's 2nd largest economy. Tiananmen (1989).

timelineEvents.name: Sumerian Civilization
timelineEvents.description: World's first civilization. Invented writing (cuneiform), the wheel, and the 60-minute hour.

timelineEvents.name: Ancient Egypt Unification
timelineEvents.description: Upper and Lower Egypt unified under Pharaoh Narmer. 3,000-year civilization begins.

timelineEvents.name: Indus Valley Civilization
timelineEvents.description: One of the earliest urban civilizations. Remarkable city planning with drainage systems.

timelineEvents.name: Ancient China – Xia Dynasty
timelineEvents.description: Legendary first dynasty of China. Beginning of Chinese dynastic civilization.

timelineEvents.name: Stonehenge Construction
timelineEvents.description: Neolithic monument built over 1,500 years. Purpose still debated.

timelineEvents.name: Minoan Civilization
timelineEvents.description: Europe's first advanced civilization on Crete. Palace of Knossos.

timelineEvents.name: Invention of Bronze
timelineEvents.description: Copper-tin alloy revolutionized tools, weapons, and civilization.

timelineEvents.name: Phoenician Alphabet
timelineEvents.description: First widely-used phonetic alphabet. Ancestor of Greek, Latin, Arabic, and Hebrew scripts.

timelineEvents.name: Bronze Age Collapse
timelineEvents.description: Sudden collapse of every major civilization in the Eastern Mediterranean.

timelineEvents.name: Mesopotamian Trade Routes
timelineEvents.description: Earliest long-distance trade networks connecting Mesopotamia to Indus Valley.

timelineEvents.name: Rise of Athens & Democracy
timelineEvents.description: Athenian democracy established by Cleisthenes. Foundation of Western political thought.

timelineEvents.name: Persian Empire (Achaemenid)
timelineEvents.description: Largest empire the world had yet seen. Cyrus the Great's rule from Egypt to India.

timelineEvents.name: Alexander the Great's Conquests
timelineEvents.description: Conquered from Greece to India in 11 years. Spread Hellenistic culture across 5.2M km².

timelineEvents.name: Roman Republic Founded
timelineEvents.description: Overthrow of monarchy. Senate and consuls established Republican governance.

timelineEvents.name: Roman Empire at Peak
timelineEvents.description: Under Trajan, Rome controlled 5M km² from Britain to Mesopotamia. ~70M people.

timelineEvents.name: Silk Road Established
timelineEvents.description: Trade network connecting China to Rome. 6,500 km of overland routes.

timelineEvents.name: Birth of Buddhism
timelineEvents.description: Siddhartha Gautama attains enlightenment. Buddhism spreads across Asia.

timelineEvents.name: Qin Dynasty – China Unified
timelineEvents.description: First Emperor Qin Shi Huang unifies China. Great Wall begun, terracotta army built.

timelineEvents.name: Fall of Rome
timelineEvents.description: Western Roman Empire falls to Germanic king Odoacer. End of ancient world.

timelineEvents.name: Han Dynasty Golden Age
timelineEvents.description: Han Dynasty rules China for 400 years. Paper invented, Silk Road flourishes.

timelineEvents.name: Carthage & Punic Wars
timelineEvents.description: Three wars between Rome and Carthage. Hannibal crosses Alps with elephants.

timelineEvents.name: Maurya Empire
timelineEvents.description: First empire to unite most of the Indian subcontinent under Chandragupta Maurya.

timelineEvents.name: Crucifixion of Jesus
timelineEvents.description: Founding event of Christianity, which would become the world's largest religion.

timelineEvents.name: Rise of Islam
timelineEvents.description: Prophet Muhammad's Hijra to Medina. Islam rapidly expands across Middle East and North Africa.

timelineEvents.name: Byzantine Empire Peak
timelineEvents.description: Under Justinian I, Byzantium reconquered Mediterranean. Hagia Sophia built.

timelineEvents.name: Viking Age
timelineEvents.description: Norse raiders, traders, and explorers reach from Baghdad to North America.

timelineEvents.name: Mongol Empire
timelineEvents.description: Genghis Khan unites Mongol tribes. Builds largest contiguous land empire ever (24M km²).

timelineEvents.name: Black Death
timelineEvents.description: Bubonic plague kills 30–60% of Europe's population (75–200 million dead).

timelineEvents.name: Crusades Begin
timelineEvents.description: 200 years of religious wars for control of the Holy Land.

timelineEvents.name: Magna Carta
timelineEvents.description: English barons force King John to accept limits on royal power. Foundation of rule of law.

timelineEvents.name: Ottoman Empire Founded
timelineEvents.description: Osman I founds dynasty that would rule for 624 years and control three continents.

timelineEvents.name: Mali Empire & Mansa Musa
timelineEvents.description: Mansa Musa's pilgrimage to Mecca. Richest person in history (adjusted ~$400B).

timelineEvents.name: Gutenberg Printing Press
timelineEvents.description: Movable type printing revolutionizes knowledge distribution. Triggers information revolution.

timelineEvents.name: Fall of Constantinople
timelineEvents.description: Ottoman Sultan Mehmed II conquers the last remnant of the Roman Empire.

timelineEvents.name: Aztec Empire
timelineEvents.description: Tenochtitlan founded on Lake Texcoco. Population reaches 200,000+.

timelineEvents.name: Inca Empire
timelineEvents.description: Largest empire in pre-Columbian Americas. 12M people across 4,000 km of Andes.

timelineEvents.name: Great Zimbabwe
timelineEvents.description: Largest stone structure in sub-Saharan Africa. Capital of a prosperous trading kingdom.

timelineEvents.name: Columbus Reaches Americas
timelineEvents.description: Christopher Columbus lands in the Bahamas. Begins the Columbian Exchange.

timelineEvents.name: Protestant Reformation
timelineEvents.description: Martin Luther's 95 Theses split Western Christianity. Reshapes European politics.

timelineEvents.name: Mughal Empire
timelineEvents.description: Babur conquers Delhi. Mughal dynasty rules India for 330 years.
